evals.name: discriminated-union-exhaustive
evals.prompt: I have a Shape type that can be circle, rectangle, or triangle. Write a function that calculates the area using a switch statement. How do I make sure I handle all cases?
evals.expected_output: Should use a `never` exhaustive check or `satisfies` to ensure all cases are handled at compile time, not just a default clause with a runtime throw.

assertions.text: Uses never type or satisfies for exhaustive checking
Explains exhaustive checking at compile time
assertions.description: Should explain how assigning to never or using satisfies catches missing cases at compile time, not just at runtime

assertions.text: Does not rely solely on a default throw for exhaustiveness

evals.name: branded-type-domain-safety
evals.prompt: I have UserId and OrderId that are both strings. I keep accidentally passing a UserId where an OrderId is expected. How do I prevent this at the type level?
evals.expected_output: Should use branded/opaque type pattern with a phantom brand field or unique symbol, not a plain type alias which provides no type safety.

assertions.text: Uses branded type pattern with brand field or unique symbol
Explains nominal typing vs structural typing
assertions.description: Should explain that TypeScript is structurally typed so plain type aliases are interchangeable, and branded types simulate nominal typing

assertions.text: Does not suggest plain type alias as the solution

evals.name: conditional-type-inference
evals.prompt: I need a utility type that extracts the resolved type from a Promise. If it's not a Promise, return the type unchanged. Write it with conditional types.
evals.expected_output: Should use the `infer` keyword with a conditional type extending Promise to extract the inner type.

assertions.text: Uses infer keyword to extract the Promise inner type

assertions.description: Should explain how conditional types distribute over union types and how infer captures the resolved type from the Promise generic parameter

evals.name: module-augmentation
evals.prompt: I'm using express and I want to add a `user` property to the Request type so I can access `req.user` in my handlers without TypeScript complaining. How do I extend the express types?
evals.expected_output: Should use `declare module` to augment the express namespace and merge the Request interface, not redeclare a standalone interface.

assertions.text: Does not redeclare a standalone Request interface without module context

assertions.description: Should explain that declare module merges declarations into the existing module's namespace, while a standalone interface creates a new unrelated type

evals.name: type-fest-vs-handrolled
evals.prompt: I need a type that makes all properties of an object readonly recursively, including nested objects. Should I write my own recursive mapped type or is there a better way?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend type-fest's ReadonlyDeep utility type rather than hand-rolling a recursive mapped type.

assertions.text: Explains when to use type-fest over hand-rolled types
assertions.description: Should explain that type-fest handles edge cases (arrays, Maps, Sets, readonly tuples) that naive recursive mapped types miss, and is well-tested
